title: Marketing Specialist
description:
**Marketing Specialist (Milano)**

**Chi siamo**

Optimal Naturals azienda operativa nel settore della cosmetica.

**Il ruolo**

Siamo alla ricerca di un/una **Marketing Specialist** brillante, proattivo e orientato ai dati per potenziare il nostro team di Milano. In questo ruolo, sarai responsabile della pianificazione, esecuzione e monitoraggio delle nostre campagne di marketing (sia digitali che tradizionali), con l'obiettivo di incrementare la brand awareness e generare lead qualificati.

**Responsabilità principali**

* Pianificare, creare e gestire campagne di **Digital Marketing** (ADV su Meta, Google Ads, LinkedIn).
* Monitorare e ottimizzare le performance delle campagne attraverso strumenti di analisi (**Google Analytics**, KPI di conversione).
* Gestire e ottimizzare i canali social aziendali, definendo il piano editoriale in collaborazione con il content team.
* Supportare la strategia **SEO/SEM** per migliorare il posizionamento del sito web.
* Collaborare alla creazione di materiali di marketing (newsletter, landing page, presentazioni aziendali).
* Analizzare i trend di mercato e le attività dei competitor per identificare nuove opportunità di crescita.

**Requisiti richiesti**

* **Esperienza:** Almeno 2\-3 anni di esperienza comprovata in ruoli di Marketing Specialist o affini (in agenzia o in azienda).
* **Formazione:** Laurea in Marketing, Comunicazione, Economia o percorsi equivalenti.
* **Competenze tecniche:**
* Ottima conoscenza di Google Ads, Meta Business Suite e Google Analytics.
* Familiarità con piattaforme di Email Marketing (es. Mailchimp, HubSpot).
* Conoscenza base dei principi SEO e CMS (WordPress).
* **Soft Skills:** Forte orientamento ai dati e ai risultati, ottime capacità comunicative e di copywriting, attitudine al lavoro di squadra e gestione dello stress.
* **Lingue:** Ottima conoscenza della lingua italiana e buona conoscenza dell'inglese (B2 o superiore).

**Cosa offriamo**

* Contratto di lavoro a tempo indeterminato.
* Ambiente di lavoro dinamico, giovane e meritocratico.
* Opportunità di crescita professionale e formazione continua.
* Smart working flessibile (es. formula 3\+2\)

**Come candidarsi**

Se pensi di essere la persona giusta, invia il tuo **CV aggiornato** e, se disponibile, un breve portfolio dei tuoi progetti di marketing di maggior successo.

Retribuzione: €28\.000,00 \- €36\.000,00 all'anno

title: Farmacista collaboratore/collaboratrice - Lainate
description:
Unisciti al nostro team e contribuisci al benessere delle persone!
  
  

Chi siamo
  
  

Farma Acquisition Holding, parte del The Boots Group, è un Retailer Farmaceutico che gestisce oltre 90 Farmacie di proprietà presenti in più di 10 regioni italiane.
  
  

Le nostre radici affondano negli anni '70 con Alleanza Farmaceutica, storica realtà della Distribuzione all'ingrosso di farmaci. Da questa esperienza è nato il nostro percorso nella gestione diretta delle Farmacie, con l'obiettivo di offrire un servizio sempre più vicino alle persone e ai bisogni di salute delle comunità locali.
  
  

Nel 2018 Boots è arrivata in Italia, portando con sé una storia iniziata nel 1849 nel Regno Unito e un'esperienza riconosciuta a livello internazionale nel campo della salute, del benessere, dell'innovazione e della qualità del servizio.
  
  

In Boots crediamo che la Farmacia sia molto più di un luogo dove dispensare farmaci: è uno spazio di ascolto, prevenzione e consulenza, in cui ogni professionista può contribuire concretamente al benessere delle persone e fare la differenza ogni giorno.
  
  

Per la nostra Farmacia di Lainate siamo alla ricerca di un/una Farmacista che desideri crescere in un ambiente dinamico, innovativo e orientato alla qualità del servizio.
  
  

Principali attività
  
  

La risorsa sarà inserita all’interno della Farmacia e contribuirà allo svolgimento delle attività professionali, operative e gestionali necessarie al corretto funzionamento del punto vendita.
  
  

Si occuperà della dispensazione di farmaci, parafarmaci e prodotti per la salute, della consulenza qualificata al cliente/paziente e della vendita assistita di dispositivi medici, integratori, prodotti dermocosmetici, articoli sanitari e prodotti per il benessere.
  
  

Parteciperà inoltre all’erogazione dei Servizi di Farmacia e della “Farmacia dei Servizi”, inclusi Servizi di screening, prevenzione, autoanalisi, prenotazioni, telemedicina e altre prestazioni attivabili secondo la normativa vigente.
  
  

La risorsa supporterà il Team nelle attività di gestione del magazzino, riordino, controllo delle scorte, verifica delle scadenze, corretta conservazione dei prodotti, utilizzo dei gestionali aziendali e gestione degli adempimenti amministrativi e documentali.
  
  

Collaborerà, inoltre, al rispetto della normativa di settore e delle procedure relative ai rapporti con ASL/ATS ed enti competenti, alla gestione dei farmaci stupefacenti, alla tracciabilità, alla farmacovigilanza e agli ulteriori adempimenti previsti per l’esercizio dell’attività farmaceutica.
  
  

Completano le attività la collaborazione con il Team di Farmacia, il rispetto delle procedure aziendali e il contributo al mantenimento di elevati standard di qualità del servizio, efficienza gestionale e continuità operativa.
  
  

Competenze e capacità richieste
  
  

Cerchiamo una persona con solida preparazione professionale, orientamento alla qualità del servizio e attenzione alla relazione con il cliente/paziente.
  
  

Sono richieste capacità di ascolto, comunicazione e consulenza, unite a precisione, affidabilità e senso di responsabilità nello svolgimento delle attività quotidiane.
  
  

Completano il profilo spirito di collaborazione, flessibilità organizzativa, attitudine al lavoro in team e interesse a contribuire all’evoluzione della farmacia come presidio sanitario di prossimità, nel rispetto degli standard professionali, aziendali e normativi.
  
  

Cosa offriamo
  
CCNL Farmacie Private
  
Contratto a tempo indeterminato
  
Full\-time
  
Formazione continua in ambito scientifico, consulenziale e gestionale
  
Sistema incentivante legato ai risultati
  
Percorsi di crescita professionale all'interno del Gruppo
  
Ambiente collaborativo e orientato al benessere delle persone
  
Work\-life balance garantito
  
  

Retribuzione e benefit

title: Sales Specialist- Monza
description:
Descrizione azienda



Grazie alle recenti acquisizioni di Juice, Rekordata, Med Computer, Med Innovations e ICT, avvenuta nel 2024, **R\-Store** è oggi il principale partner Apple in Italia e tra i più rilevanti al mondo. Con headquarter a Napoli e una presenza in 18 regioni, l’azienda conta 100 negozi, 900 collaboratori e un fatturato di 600 milioni di euro. Oltre al canale retail consumer, supportato da personale altamente qualificato, R\-Store offre soluzioni su misura per aziende, scuole, università e pubblica amministrazione, garantendo un servizio post\-vendita completo come Centro di Assistenza Autorizzato Apple. Dal 2022 il gruppo ha diversificato il business entrando nei settori della Cyber Security e dei dispositivi ricondizionati, fino ad approdare nel 2024 al mondo della fotografia d'autore con l'apertura del primo Leica Store a Napoli



Posizione



In ambito Retail (Vendita, Service, Customer Relation) cerchiamo Apple Sales Specialist per il nostro punto vendita di **Monza.**


**Cosa farai:**


* Accoglierai ogni cliente con un approccio consulenziale, trasformando ogni interazione in un’esperienza significativa.
* Capirai i bisogni, proporrai soluzioni integrate – prodotti, accessori, protezioni, servizi finanziari e noleggio operativo.
* Cura dell'esposizione dei prodotti nella show room secondo il planogramma schema espositivo) e relativi aggiornamenti, previsti da Apple e da R\-Store.
* Aggiornamento e corretta esposizione del materiale di merchandising.
* Ti relazionerai anche con clienti business e professionisti, offrendo soluzioni su misura grazie alla collaborazione con Rekordata, la società del gruppo dedicata al mondo aziendale
* Ti formerai continuamente grazie alla nostra Academy e ai programmi ufficiali Apple, per diventare un vero esperto della Customer Experience.
* Primo supporto nella risoluzione di problematica di utilizzo dispositivi Apple.


Requisiti


**Chi cerchiamo?**


* Persone curiose con passione per la tecnologia e soprattutto del mondo Apple
* Capacità di ascolto, empatia e orientamento al risultato.
* Esperienza in ambito Retail e preferibilmente nell’elettronica di consumo.
* Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese.


Altre informazioni


**Cosa offriamo:**


* Retribuzione fissa (RAL) e bonus mensile fino al 30%, legato a obiettivi chiari misurabili.
* Crescita professionale in un’azienda solida, in espansione e con valori condivisi.
* Un sistema di incentivazione meritocratico basato su risultati misurabili.
* Percorsi di formazione certificata Apple, con sviluppo di competenze su vendita consulenziale, comunicazione e customer experience.
* Opportunità di crescita reale all’interno del gruppo R\-Store, sia nel canale retail che in aree specialistiche come business, education e digital solutions.
* Inquadramento: RAL: 23\.000 \- 25\.000 \- 5/ 4 livello CCNL Commercio e Terziario

**Perché R\-Store**



R\-Store come principale Premium Partner del brand, è a capo di un ecosistema strategico che unisce eccellenze complementari: Rekordata, che trasforma il business con soluzioni tecnologiche su misura, e Clever, il cuore green del gruppo dedicato ai prodotti ricondizionati e alla sostenibilità.



Inoltre, entrerai a far parte di un ambiente in cui le persone rappresentano il valore più importante. Crediamo che il successo di ogni progetto, decisione e innovazione nasca dal contributo di chi lo rende possibile ogni giorno. Per questo mettiamo collaboratori, clienti e partner al centro di ogni processo, valorizzandone competenze, idee e potenziale. In R\-Store promuoviamo una cultura basata sull’ascolto, sulla collaborazione e sulla crescita reciproca, perché siamo convinti che siano le persone a fare davvero la differenza.

title: Telespazio - Tecnico specialista in Networking
description:
**Job Description:**

**Closer to Space, closer to the Future: entra in Telespazio – Divisione Spazio Leonardo!**

**Leonardo** è un gruppo industriale internazionale, tra le principali realtà mondiali nell’Aerospazio, Difesa e Sicurezza che realizza capacità tecnologiche multidominio in ambito Elicotteri, Velivoli, Aerostrutture, Elettronica, Cyber Security e Spazio. All’interno della Divisione Spazio di Leonardo, **Telespazio joint venture tra Leonardo (67%) e Thales (33%)**, è leader globale nel settore delle soluzioni e dei servizi satellitari. Con oltre 3\.400 professionisti distribuiti tra le sue sedi in Europa e nel mondo, Telespazio gestisce una rete internazionale di Centri Spaziali e Teleporti, fornendo soluzioni innovative per Servizi di Geoinformazione, Sistemi e Operazioni Satellitari e Comunicazioni Satellitari.

*Unisciti a noi mentre continuiamo a superare i confini del possibile nello Spazio.*


All’interno dell’Area Servizi di **Teleporto del Lario**, stiamo ricercando una risorsa da aggiungere al team specialistico dedicato prevalentemente alla gestione di **Networking**, di piattaforme digitali ed impianti ricetrasmittenti per le **telecomunicazioni satellitari**.


La risorsa si occuperà delle seguenti attività:

* Garantire la disponibilità dei dati di configurazione dei prodotti di competenza
* Predisporre e garantire la funzionalità dei tools di monitoria per le Piattaforme e servizi di competenza.
* Redigere documentazione tecnica e procedure operative necessarie sia per la gestione operativa sia per la conformità agli standard della qualità applicabili ( EN9100\) anche in lingua inglese.
* Garantire la gestione dei servizi operativi sulle piattaforme di competenza
* Svolgere attività di supporto operativo avanzato di secondo livello (L2\) su infrastruttura, comprese piattaforme digitali TV/TLC Hardware, i livelli di virtualizzazione (ad es. VMware vSphere), le macchine virtuali e data center, garantendo la continuità del servizio, la stabilità e la prevenzione proattività dei problemi.
* Pianificare ed eseguire attività di manutenzione sia Hardware che Software sulle infrastrutture reti, piattaforme digitali, data center utilizzate per l’erogazione dei servizi satellitari gestiti dal Centro di controllo del Lario.
* Supporto in attività di engineering nella definizione di requisiti, updating ed upgrading di Hardware, Software, sistemi operativi Windows Server, Linux, Applicativi e virtualizzazione.
* Supporto On\-call Service per la risoluzione di problematiche tecnico operative degli impianti di competenza.
* Reporting periodici ed occasionali anche in lingua inglese.

**Requisiti:**

* Diploma di scuola superiore o Laurea Triennale con indirizzo in Informatica e Telecomunicazioni.
* Mid\-Level (3\-5 anni di esperienza nel settore della gestione delle reti IP).
* Teoria di base delle Telecomunicazioni: Antenne, Propagazione, Teoria dei segnali, tecniche di modulazione ed accesso a satellite, Fibre ottiche
* Solide conoscenze di progettazione, configurazione e gestione di reti IP
* Monitoraggio proattivo delle performance di rete e troubleshooting di livello L2/L3
* Sicurezza informatica: buona conoscenza di firewalling e segmentazione di rete, best practices di sicurezza.


	+ Configurazione e gestione di apparati di rete:
	
	
		- Router e switch
		- Sistemi firewall e VPN
	+ Implementazione e manutenzione di:
	
	
		- Routing dinamico (BGP)
		- Segmentazione di rete (VLAN)
* Supporto nella gestione di reti multi\-site e infrastrutture ibride (fibra \+ satellite)
* Conoscenza dei principali strumenti di monitoraggio utilizzati nel campo del networking, connettività terrestre e satellitare.
* Flessibilità, predisposizione al cambiamento, Iniziativa, creatività, innovazione, proattività
* Esperienza con ITIL (Incident, Change, Problem Management)
* Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese sia parlata che scritta. Livello di riferimento C1 certificato
* Gradita conoscenza delle tipologie di trasmissione dei dati nel settore del broadcasting.
* Disponibilità a trasferte

**Cosa offriamo:**

* Il nostro contratto collettivo del lavoro di riferimento è il CCNL per Industria Metalmeccanica Privata e della Installazione di Impianti;
* Modalità di lavoro: in sede;
* Categoria contrattuale: Impiegato;
* Tipologia contrattuale: tempo indeterminato
* Tredicesima mensilità;
* Premialità legata ai risultati di Business;
* Buoni Welfare dal valore di 250 euro annui;
* Buoni pasto 8,50€ da utilizzare presso mensa interna o per spese esterne
* Offriamo percorsi di formazione continua e percorsi di sviluppo professionale per valorizzare e rafforzare sia le competenze tecniche sia le soft skill.

**L’azienda si riserva la possibilità di effettuare valutazioni in merito al livello di inquadramento e alla proposta**

title: Formulation Scientist, Manufacturing Sciences
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.  

  

DESCRIPTION:  

Join our team at Thermo Fisher Scientific as a Manufacturing Sciences Scientist I, where you'll contribute to our mission of enabling customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. In this role, you'll support manufacturing operations by developing, optimizing and implementing production processes while ensuring compliance with quality standards. You'll collaborate with cross\-functional teams to manufacture critical products for diagnostics, research, and therapeutic applications. The position offers opportunities to enhance processes, solve technical challenges, and directly impact patient care through high\-quality manufacturing.  

  

REQUIREMENTS:  

* Bachelor's degree required, no prior experience required in a GMP environment
* Preferred Fields of Study: Chemistry, Biochemistry, Chemical Engineering, Biotechnology, or related scientific field
* Strong understanding of analytical methods, quality control testing, and documentation practices
* Demonstrated proficiency in standard laboratory techniques and equipment operation
* Experience with chromatography, spectroscopy, and other analytical instrumentation
* Working knowledge of GMP, GDP, and other relevant regulatory requirements

* Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and laboratory information systems

Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. is the world leader in serving science, with annual revenue of approximately $40 billion. Our Mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. Whether our customers are accelerating life sciences research, solving sophisticated analytical challenges, growing efficiency in their laboratories, improving patient health through diagnostics or the development and manufacture of life\-changing therapies, we are here to support them. Our global organization of more than 100,000 colleagues delivers an outstanding combination of innovative technologies, purchasing convenience and pharmaceutical services through our industry\-leading brands, including Thermo Scientific, Applied Biosystems, Invitrogen, Fisher Scientific, Unity Lab Services, Patheon and PPD. Monza site is an outstanding pharmaceutical contract manufacturing and development facility, specialized in aseptic injectable drugs, with a growing focus on biologics. Joining more than 1800 colleagues on site, the selected candidate will experience an excellent, international, and fast\-growing environment.

  

We are looking for a QC Chemical Analyst!

 **Position Summary**:


The ideal candidate will be responsible for conducting Quality Control testing of drug products in alignment with the shipping schedule and production requirements, ensuring the delivery of high\-quality customer service.

 **Responsibilities:**

* Conduct analysis on incoming, in\-process, and release samples in compliance with GMP standards to support manufacturing processes, following the instructions provided by the Team Leader, SOPs, and analytical procedures.
* Prepare and handle documentation (protocols, reports, methods) in accordance with current GMP, SOPs, and guidelines, ensuring proper adherence to regulatory standards.
* Support instrument calibration following SOPs and protocols.
* Play an active role in the Company’s dedication to Process improvement and efficiency in QC operations to increase safety, quality and performance.
* Support other member of QA \& QC team to achieve timelines set.
* Operate any equipment and conduct your day to duties according to current procedures and appropriate Health and Safety precautions.

**Minimum Requirements/Qualifications:**

* Degree in Chemistry, Biotechnology or other Scientific field.
* Previous experience in GMP QC Laboratory and in performing common chemical analysis (HPLC, UV, GC, TOC, PH, KF). Experience in performing biochemical analyses is a plus.
* Proficiency in English and Italian languages.
* Good knowledge of Office Suite and software Empower.

**Soft Skills:**

* Good time\-management.
* Good attention to detail, accuracy and work to tight deadlines.
* Can multi\-task and adapt to changing priorities.
* Efficient and self\-motivated, willing to learn all aspects of Quality Control.
* Able to communicate effectively at all levels.
* Ability to work in a team.


Thermo Fisher Scientific is an Equal Opportunity Employer.  

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, religion, color, national or ethnic origin, citizenship, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, genetic information, veteran status, age or disability status.


Join us!

description:

Other**Environmental Conditions**


Laboratory Setting, Office, Some degree of PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) required (safety glasses, gowning, gloves, lab coat, ear plugs etc.)**Job Description**


When you’re part of Thermo Fisher Scientific, you’ll do challenging work, and join a team that values performance, quality and innovation. As part of a successful, growing global organization you will be encouraged to perform at your best. With revenues of more than $35 billion and the largest investment in R\&D in the industry, we give our people the resources and chances to create significant contributions to the world.


Monza offers compliance with controlled drug regulations in Europe and the U.S. as well as disposable manufacturing technologies and fully integrated secondary packaging services, and has received regulatory approval from various international regulatory inspection agencies, including: ANVISA, JP PMDA, US FDA, EMA, GCC States, Canada, Russia, Taiwan, Turkey. The Monza site currently supplies over 20 countries – all the major international markets, including the U.S., Europe and Asia Pacific.


The candidate will join the PDS Analytical Development\&GMP Department in a new built of Pharmaceutical Department (PDS).


Depending on the indications received from the Team Leader, he carries out the tasks and activities listed below for which he has been enabled by the appropriate training, in compliance with company and departmental SOPs and current safety regulations


The analytical Scientist should be experienced in the use of numerous analytical tools used for small and large molecule analysis, such as HPLC, GC, IR, UV\- Spectrophotometer and other minor equipments. This work will be supporting product development efforts (both API and Drug Product) along with Stability testing and other endeavors requested by the client. This role will require experience in working in a cGMP environment. The candidate should be familiar with using Empower software. MS Office experience is required.


Responsible for testing of raw materials (especially API), in\-process \& finished product, formulations and according to SOPs. He takes care activities as Analytical Method Development, Analytical Method Transfer and Validation, routine and stability analysis. Compiles data for documentation of test procedures, prepares reports. Ensures all testing processes, monitoring, and departmental documentation meet SOPs and cGMP regulatory standards. Contributes to the development of new concepts, techniques, and standards. Involved with Assists in the preparation of CoAs.


Documents all experimental data in accordance with Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original and Accurate (ALCOA). Independently carries out analysis using equipment as HPLC and UPLC, FT\-IR, UV\-Spectrophotometer, Viscosimeter following established procedures Analytical Methods \& Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs). Works Proficiency with Empower, MS Word \& Excel Software. Performs basic troubleshooting to overcome system suitability failures. Recognizes and reports unexpected or Out Of Specification (OOS) results immediately to the Supervisor and conducts laboratory investigations under direction. Independently prepares raw data packages (notebook copies and Empower reports) for clients.


You will be shadowing and/or assisting in the preparation of scientific documents such as methods, protocols, reports, method development summaries, analytical methods, SOPs and other similar documentation.


Coordinate a group of analysts who conduct tests related to Analytical Method Development/Transfer/Validation, in\-process samples, finished products, and stability samples. Interacts with internal and external clients to set timelines, understand work requests, define and resolve issues, assign and distribute work, and communicate results. Participates and contributes to safety programs, department and client meetings, and in maintaining an orderly and safe laboratory environment.


Recognizes and solves analytical and product problems. Conducts and documents investigations. Reviews laboratory documentation for calculation, entry and GMP compliance. Solves complex, non\-routine analytical and product problems independently. Oversees the documentation of laboratory experimentation in protocols and technical reports.


Takes care of the kitting activities for the execution of the department analyzes on the basis of internal planning in accordance with the methods in use.


Takes care of keeping the materials in good condition for their use in the analytical field, guaranteeing their conservation under the conditions indicated in the corresponding documentation.


Collaborates in the evaluation of the materials to be purchased for the assigned analytical activity and related documentation and, if designated, takes care of the preparation of the corresponding purchase request.


Takes care of the tools, materials and work environment, promptly communicating any anomalies to the Team Leader


In compliance with company procedures, it takes care of collecting waste and laboratory waste appropriately.

**Requirements:**

* Required: Bachelor’s degree in chemical pharmaceutical, biology, chemistry, biotechnology or related field or equivalent work experience and knowledge
* Office program
* In\-depth knowledge of the most common analytical techniques and required instrumentation (HPLC, FT\-IR, UV\-VIS, Physical tests etc..)
* Stability and routine tests on APIs, finished products
* Experience in Analytical Method Development/Transfer and Validation
* Knowledge of company procedures and GMPs
* Knowledge of notions and laboratory computer programs
* BS required/MS preferred in a science related field and 2\-3 years of laboratory experiences, preferably in a pharmaceutical, biotechnology, healthcare, research, or academic environment
**L****anguages****:**

* English
**Personality****traits:**

* Flexibility
* Good organization and planning skills
* Listening and communication skills
* Ability to work in a team

title: Associate Medical Director, Cardiology , with Metabolic Diseases and Nephrology Experience
description:
**Job Overview**


The Associate Medical Director will provide medical, clinical and scientific advisory expertise to all IQVIA divisions as requested. Participate in all aspects of Medical Science involvement on assigned trials. Serve as a medical expert during project delivery lifecycle. Provide therapeutic and medical expertise to business development activities.


**Essential Functions**


*Medical Monitoring:*


* Primarily serves as Global Medical Advisor on assigned projects.
* Serves as Scientific Advisor and provides guidance to Project Leaders on the medical and scientific aspects of assigned projects.
* Provides medical support to investigative sites and project staff for protocol\-related issues including protocol clarifications, inclusion/exclusion determinations, and issues of patient safety and/or eligibility.
* Performs medical review of the protocol, Investigative Drug Brochure (IDB), and/or Case Report Forms (CRFs).
* Provides therapeutic area/indication training for the project clinical team.
* Attends and presents at Investigator Meetings.
* Performs review and clarification of trial\-related Adverse Events (AEs).
* May perform medical case review of Serious Adverse Events (SAEs), including review of case documentation and patient narrative, in collaboration with the Pharmacovigilance department.
* May provide medical support for the Analysis of Similar Events (AOSE), in collaboration with or on behalf of Pharmacovigilance department.
* May perform medical review of adverse event coding.
* Performs review of the Clinical Study Report (CSR) and patient narratives.
* Attends Kick\-Off meetings, weekly team meetings, and client meetings, as needed or requested.
* Available 24/7 to respond urgent protocol \-related questions from investigative sites, in accordance with local labor laws.
* May require regular travel (15%\-20%)

**Qualifications \& Requirements**


* Medical Degree from an accredited and internationally recognized medical school with a curriculum relevant to general medical education, along with a medical license, or equivalent, from the country or region in which he/she resides and works is required, plus a minimum of 5 years' experience in clinical medicine, in addition to clinical trials experience as an investigator or in the Pharma, CRO, or Biotech industry.
* Robust and current knowledge of scientific, clinical, regulatory, commercial and competitive landscape in applicable therapeutic area.
* Knowledge of applicable federal and local regulations and guidelines pertaining to clinical research.
* Business Acumen.
* Board Certified in Cardiology.
* Knowledge of cardiology, metabolic diseases and nephrology.
* Experience or knowledge in Phase I studies or early clinical development.
* Fluent English.
* Extensive communication skills.

IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com


IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.


The potential base pay range for this role, when annualized, is €73,900\.00 \- €125,900\.00\. The actual base pay offered may vary based on a number of factors including job\-related qualifications such as knowledge, skills, education, and experience; location; and/or schedule (full or part\-time). Dependent on the position offered, incentive plans, bonuses, and/or other forms of compensation may be offered, in addition to a range of health and welfare and/or other benefits.

title: Executive Director, General Partner (Biopharma Account Lead)
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


At Thermo Fisher Scientific, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life \- enabling our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while taking science a step beyond through research, development and delivery of life\-changing therapies.


With clinical trials conducted in 100\+ countries and ongoing development of novel frameworks for clinical research through our PPD clinical research portfolio, our work spans laboratory, digital and decentralized clinical trial services. Your determination to deliver quality and accuracy will improve health outcomes that people and communities depend on – now and in the future.


We are looking to hire an **Executive Director, General Partner– Biopharma.**


As the Executive Director General Partner, you will be accountable for the strategic direction across assigned developing partnership account(s) working closely with a commercial Group Leader to focus on accelerating the growth and expanding the relationship of biopharmaceutical customers into strategic partnership accounts. This role will provide consultation, direction, and leadership to functional leads and employees to ensure commercial excellence, consistency, quality, innovation and learning across the portfolio of work inclusive of operational and financial targets. Provides an important interface with the client and with other departments internally on all aspects of assigned partnership account(s).

**How will you make an impact:**  

  

You will enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer by helping our clients and sponsors to understand how our clinical development service offering enables them to deliver life\-changing therapies to their patients more quickly.


What will you do:

 **Relationship Management**: Cultivate and expand deep relationships at the highest levels within the account working in close coordination with your direct reports and other departments. Establish and grow customer loyalty and Customer Delight, including proactively engaging customers with insights on industry trends and potential challenges to position proactive innovative solutions to overcome them. Drive engagement between Executive Sponsor and other senior executives within broader Thermo Fisher leadership to foster deep trust and confidence in CRG’s offerings while gaining business insights to accelerate expansion. Make connections and identify new account areas for potential footprint expansion. Build and strengthen client advocacy and engagement at the highest account organizational levels. Establish robust CRG stakeholder management across all business units.  

  

**Account Ownership:** Drive team to achieve target within Clinical Development Services Division and all Clinical Research business units. Expand existing commercial presence to grow account into strategic partner. Central point of contact for internal and external communication across all CRG. Proactively engages and drive internal stakeholders across the TMO organization (CRG, PSG, other groups), direct reports, and business partners (Finance, Legal, Sales, Operational Delivery, Governance) to ensure operational, financial and commercial goals are achieved. Coaches team and is actively involved on proposals and bid defenses and owns partnership pursuits efforts within account. Develop and maintain active account plan and demonstrate progress against planned strategies. Proactively solicits feedback from internal and external stakeholders to capture business intelligence for all account team members to share intelligence, best practices, and drive competitive differentiation.  

  

**Develops Growth Strategies:** Identifies key areas of strategic expansion within account and drives proactive selling across all business units within CRG and across PSG. Collaborates with therapeutic unit leadership to develop strategies and execute against account growth plan with emphasis on enterprise level view. Develops innovative commercial solutions (including commercial constructs, financial incentive structures, operational models) and proactively presents to customer to address existing challenges. Proactively identify and address financial, operational, relationship and quality concerns aligned with customer needs while emphasizing TMO’s market differentiation. Develops strong working relationships with internal operational, commercial and finance partners to execute according to plan. Prioritize top revenue generation and maximize to increase market penetration.  

  

**Operational quality and efficiency:** Enterprise champion collaborating effectively with direct reports and operational colleagues to ensure exquisite operational delivery, exceed operational milestones while maintaining expected levels of quality and rigor. Coach Dev Ops and others to proactively address potential challenges and issues while optimizing existing operating models to support more efficient ways of working. Drive incumbency on account,  

  

**Innovation:** Leverage collective relationship to identify customer needs to inform innovation and investment strategies. Demonstrates a deep understanding of the client’s market position, needs, and competitive landscape consistently with ability to influence internal commercial, technological and operational innovation to proactively reduce gaps and drive transformation. Deeply understand industry trends to inform clients. Challenge enterprise on client needs to force innovation discussion and application to proactively reduce gaps and drive process improvements.  

  

**Team Management:** Recruit, train, and manage the high\-performing account team, including business development and development operations. Foster a collaborative and motivated environment focused on accountability for results, client advocacy and innovative problem solving by providing rigorous oversight, coaching and guidance to maximize individual and team performance using Challenger methodology. Ensure consistent delivery for each business development representative of top\-line sales goals and targeted profit contribution. Drives commercial execution excellence. Correctly use Salesforce and enforce all sales administrative tasks.

**How will you get here:**

* A bachelor’s degree in business administration, sales, or a related field is the required minimum education; MBA or advanced degree is preferred.
* Previous experience that provides the knowledge, skills, and abilities to perform the job comparable to 15\+ years; strong preference for experience in a CRO, Pharmaceutical or Medical Device sales industry.
* Proven sales history with breadth of domestic geographic responsibility and 5\+ years of account management experience. In some cases, an equivalency, consisting of a combination of appropriate education, training and/or directly related experience, will be considered sufficient for an individual to meet the requirements of the role.

**Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:**

* Demonstrated success in a similar commercial leadership role that has consistently achieved and exceeded commercial targets.
* Robust **Clinical Operations** experience
* **In\-depth knowledge of the CRO industry and clinical trial processes**, including regulatory requirements and best practices. Strong understanding of complex technical requirements and the ability to effectively communicate technical information to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders.
* Excellent leadership and communication skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate, cultivate and influence stakeholders across functions and at all levels. Executive presence with ability to build and foster senior level customer relationships.
* Demonstrates robust judgment, problem solving and decision making; Ability to apply critical and analytical thinking and business acumen to develop effective solutions.
* A “big\-picture” thinker with the ability to contribute meaningfully to strategic discussions while also remaining attuned to the details required for successful operational execution.
* Ability to create constructive tension with customers by identifying customer challenges and potential gaps, designing customized solutions and messaging to influence customer behavior and influence the buying process
* Understanding of management, planning, organizing and resourcing skills and managing priorities. A high tolerance for ambiguity and the ability to navigate complexity
* Great communicator and negotiator, able to develop shared perspectives and achieve actionable results through others.
* Ability to handle complex situations and multiple responsibilities simultaneously mixing long term projects with the urgency of immediate demands on the operations.
* Demonstrated analytical skills with the ability to judge situations accurately and quickly, grasping complexities, identifying key issues and applying sound judgment.
* Ability to quickly and effectively build credibility and strong relationships at all levels
* Strategic thinker with ability to uncover and understand customer needs and construct novel enterprise level solutions to address customer needs
* Must show the ability to demonstrate the Thermo Fisher values (The Four I’s) – Integrity, Intensity, Innovation, and Involvement

**Working Conditions and Environment:**

* Work is performed in an office environment with exposure to electrical office equipment.
* Travel, both domestic and international.
* Interaction with clients/associates required.
* Long varied hours required occasionally.

**What we offer:**


At PPD Clinical Research Services we hire the best, develop ourselves and each other, and recognise the power of being one team. We understand that you will want to grow both professionally and personally throughout your career, and therefore at PPD you will benefit from an award\-winning learning and development programme, ensuring you reach your potential.


As well as being rewarded a competitive salary, we have an extensive benefits package based around the health and well\-being of our employees. We have a flexible working culture, where PPD Clinical Research Services truly value a work\-life balance. We’ve grown sustainably year on year but continue to offer a collaborative environment, with teams of colleagues eager to share expertise and have fun together. We are a global organisation but with a local feel.


Our Mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. Watch as our colleagues explain 5 reasons to work with us. As one team of 100,000\+ colleagues, we share a common set of values \- Integrity, Intensity, Innovation and Involvement \- working together to accelerate research, solve complex scientific challenges, drive technological innovation and support patients in need. \#StartYourStory with PPD, part of Thermo Fisher Scientific, where diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives are valued.


Apply today! http://jobs.thermofisher.com

Per rafforzare il nostro team, cerchiamo una risorsa per l'attività di Head of Legal \& Compliance che sappia coniugare rigore regolamentare e visione strategica, operando a diretto riporto del Vertice Aziendale e degli Organi di Controllo.
  
  

La risorsa sarà il punto di riferimento unico per le tematiche legali, societarie e regolamentari della SIM. Avrà la responsabilità di garantire la piena conformità delle attività aziendali, combinando Funzione Compliance e di Presidio Legal \& Contrattualistica.
  
  

**Principali Responsabilità:**  

**Governance \& Compliance:** Definizione e monitoraggio del Piano di Compliance; esecuzione di risk assessment e verifiche periodiche sui processi interni.
  
**Regulatory \& Advisory:** Monitoraggio dell'evoluzione normativa (Consob, Banca d’Italia, MiFID II) e supporto ex\-ante al management e all'Investment Team per il lancio di nuovi servizi e linee di business.
  
**Contrattualistica \& Accordi:** Redazione e manutenzione della contrattualistica retail/corporate e negoziazione di accordi strategici con partner, broker, outsourcer e fornitori.
  
**Societario \& Tutela:** Supporto legale a CdA e Collegio Sindacale (verbali, deleghe, reportistica); gestione dei registri obbligatori (reclami/conflitti) e del precontenzioso.
  
**Diffusione della cultura di conformità:** Pianificare ed erogare sessioni di formazione interna per il personale, predisponendo il relativo materiale didattico al fine di recepire le evoluzioni normative e promuovere comportamenti operativi etici e coerenti con i principi di correttezza verso la clientela.
  
  

**Requisiti tecnici:**  

**Esperienza:** 8\-12 anni in ruoli analoghi (Compliance, Legal, Control) presso SIM, SGR, Banche o Studi Legali specializzati nel settore finanziario.
  
Solida conoscenza di TUF, MiFID II, Regolamento Intermediari Consob (Product Governance, Best Execution, Inducements) e normative AML/GDPR.
  
Familiarità con firme elettroniche, documentazione precontrattuale e gestione dei reclami.
  
  

**Background:** Laurea in Giurisprudenza o Economia/Finanza (l'abilitazione forense è un plus).
  
  

Completano il profilo autorevolezza, indipendenza di giudizio e un approccio pragmatico capace di trasformare i vincoli normativi in processi operativi fluidi e scalabili.
  
  

A tutela dell'autonomia della funzione, il ruolo prevede un doppio riporto:
  
  

**Funzionale:** al Consiglio di Amministrazione (per le attività di Compliance).
  
  

**Gerarchico:** al CEO / Direzione Generale (per le attività Legal e societarie).
  
  

Perché scegliere Reversal SIM?
  
**Impatto e Visione Strategica:** realtà dinamica dove la l’expertise legale e di compliance sarà un reale fattore abilitante per il business, partecipando direttamente ai processi decisionali a fianco del Top Management.
  
**Eccellenza e Innovazione:** ambiente stimolante che valorizza l'approccio pragmatico e la digitalizzazione dei processi, lontano dalle logiche burocratiche dei grandi gruppi tradizionali.
  
**Crescita:** società in continua crescita ed evoluzione parte di un gruppo quotato. Realtà che nel panorama italiano del wealth management si colloca sempre più come leader. E si distingue per un approccio avanzato alla consulenza finanziaria applicando le metodologie di asset allocation e costruzione di portafoglio tipiche dei grandi investitori istituzionali, integrando competenze globali.
  
Il contratto offerto per questa posizione è a tempo indeterminato e la retribuzione prevista per questa posizione è compresa tra EUR 40\.000 e 55\.000 lordi annui.
  
L'inquadramento verrà definito in fase di selezione sulla base del profilo e in applicazione del CCNL Commercio.
  
La sede di lavoro è Milano e si richiede la presenza fissa in ufficio.
  
  

Posizione aperta a candidature senza distinzione di genere, ai sensi del D.Lgs. 198/2006\. L'azienda promuove le pari opportunità e valorizza la diversità in tutte le sue forme.

title: Junior Process Management Engineer
description:
TXT Group is an international, IT end\-to\-end provider of consultancy, software solutions and services, supporting the digital transformation of customers’ products and core processes. With a proprietary software portfolio and deep expertise in vertical domains, TXT Group operates across different markets, with a growing footprint in Aerospace, Aviation, Defense, Industrial, Government and Fintech. The holding company, TXT e\-Solutions, has been listed on the Italian Stock Exchange \- STAR segment (TXT.MI) \- since July 2000\. TXT Group headquartered in Milan and has subsidiaries in Italy, Germany, the United Kingdom, France, Switzerland and the United States of America.
  
  

**Junior Process Management Engineer:**  

TXT e\-Tech, part of the TXT Group, is an international technology partner specialized in the development of innovative solutions for the Aerospace, Defence, and High\-Tech sectors. Within the Embedded Systems for Aerospace Applications Business Unit, we are looking for a Junior Process Management Engineer to contribute to the definition, implementation, and monitoring of software and hardware certification processes.
  
Main Activities
  
  

The selected person will be involved in the following activities:
  
supporting the definition and update of development and certification processes;
  
monitoring compliance with regulatory and procedural requirements;
  
managing process and certification documentation;
  
handling requirements management and requirements traceability activities;
  
supporting Design Assurance and Functional Safety processes;
  
collaborating with software, hardware, and quality assurance teams;
  
participating in internal audits and compliance activities.
  
Requirements
  
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Aerospace Engineering, Aeronautical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics Engineering, or related fields.
  
Experience
  
1 to 2 years of experience in roles related to process engineering, quality assurance, systems engineering, compliance, or regulated environments.
  
Technical Skills
  
Good knowledge of aeronautical systems.
  
Experience in requirements management and traceability.
  
Knowledge of the software and/or hardware development lifecycle.
  
Knowledge of at least one programming language (C, C\+\+, C\#, Java, JavaScript, Python, Ada, or equivalent).
  
Ability to work with technical documentation and regulatory requirements.
  
Nice to Have
  
Familiarity with DO\-178C and/or DO\-254 standards.
  
Knowledge of Design Assurance and Functional Safety principles.
  
Experience with requirements management tools.
  
Familiarity with airworthiness and compliance processes.
  
Experience in Aerospace, Avionics, or Safety\-Critical Systems environments.
  
Soft Skills
  
Ability to work independently on assigned tasks.
  
Quality\-oriented mindset and continuous improvement approach.
  
Good organizational and priority management skills.
  
Ability to collaborate effectively within multidisciplinary teams.
  
Accuracy, proactivity, and results orientation.
  
Written and spoken technical English at B2 level.
  
What We Offer
  
Opportunities for professional growth within a rapidly expanding company undergoing continuous evolution, in an international and dynamic environment.
  
Continuous training paths on technical and project\-related topics.
  
Corporate benefits, including health insurance, corporate welfare, meal vouchers, and employee discounts available within the Group.
  
The salary range for this position is between EUR 28,000 and EUR 32,000 gross per year.
  
Contract level and compensation will be defined during the selection process, based on the profile and in accordance with the Italian Metalworking Collective Labour Agreement (CCNL Metalmeccanico).
  
  

This position is open to all applicants regardless of gender, in accordance with Legislative Decree 198/2006\. The company promotes equal opportunities and values diversity in all its forms.

title: Payroll M&A Intern | Milano
description:
**Payroll M\&A Intern \| Milano**

  


**The chance to build a better future is right in front of you**

  


**Do Work That Matters \- Il tuo ruolo e le tue responsabilità**

  



KPMG Fides Servizi di Amministrazione offre servizi professionali nei processi amministrativi, contabili e fiscali. Svolge un ruolo fondamentale nella riprogettazione dei modelli di reporting e nell'armonizzazione dei processi di contabilità gestionale.

  



KPMG Fides conta oltre 300 professionisti distribuiti in quattro sedi ed è in continua crescita. In qualità di società italiana del network KPMG specializzata in servizi professionali per i processi amministrativi, contabili e fiscali, KPMG Accounting supporta importanti aziende italiane e internazionali nelle attività di amministrazione, finanza e controllo. L'azienda contribuisce a individuare nuove opportunità, ridisegnare i modelli di reporting e armonizzare i processi di contabilità gestionale.

  



In KPMG l'obiettivo è investire sui giovani talenti, fornendo loro solide competenze per diventare professionisti affidabili e di fiducia. Entrando a far parte di uno dei team Accounting di KPMG, collaborerai a stretto contatto con aziende leader, supportandole attraverso un approccio integrato alla compliance amministrativa, traducendo i requisiti legislativi, normativi e procedurali in processi contabili, fiscali e legali.

  



Nello specifico, entrando a far parte del team di Milano, ti occuperai di attività di **Payroll e M\&A (Fusioni e Acquisizioni)**, tra cui:

  


* Analisi approfondita delle principali tematiche relative al diritto del lavoro, alla previdenza sociale e alla fiscalità.
* Monitoraggio costante della normativa italiana e internazionale in materia contrattuale, previdenziale e fiscale.
* Supporto nell'analisi delle problematiche retributive e contributive del personale coinvolto in operazioni straordinarie d'impresa.
* Supporto nell'elaborazione mensile delle paghe e nella gestione degli adempimenti annuali e periodici.
* Contributo al processo di erogazione del servizio payroll (reportistica, ecc.).

  



  

**Come As You Are \- Le tue competenze e caratteristiche**

  



Questa opportunità è ideale se possiedi:

  


* Laurea in discipline economiche o giuridiche.
* Solida esperienza nell'ambito payroll e contributi, preferibilmente maturata presso studi professionali.
* Formazione adeguata e costante aggiornamento sulla normativa del lavoro e sulla sua corretta applicazione.
* Ottima conoscenza del pacchetto Microsoft Office, in particolare Excel.
* Flessibilità, eccellenti capacità relazionali e attitudine al lavoro di squadra.
* Ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese.

  




**Your Future With Us \- Cosa troverai in KPMG**

  


* Un ambiente di lavoro inclusivo ed equo, che rispetta, valorizza e promuove la diversità.
* Modalità di lavoro ibrida per favorire un sano equilibrio tra vita privata e professionale.
* Programmi avanzati di formazione e sviluppo professionale per mantenere competenze sempre aggiornate.
* Un contesto internazionale e progetti innovativi.
* Iniziative dedicate al benessere mentale e fisico.

L’opportunità prevede un inserimento in stage o in assunzione con inquadramento ai sensi del CCNL Commercio e RAL di ingresso: € 24\.000, determinata sulla base di criteri oggettivi quali il titolo di studio e la valutazione del profilo rispetto ai requisiti della posizione.



In ottemperanza alla normativa vigente, non raccogliamo né utilizziamo informazioni sulle retribuzioni percepite in rapporti di lavoro attuali o precedenti. Le nostre procedure di selezione sono, inoltre, improntate a criteri oggettivi e neutrali rispetto al genere.

  


**Together for Better – I nostri valori e il nostro impegno**

  



Leadership, lavoro di squadra, rispetto per ogni individuo, apprendimento continuo, trasparenza, impegno verso la comunità e integrità: questi sono i valori di KPMG, che creano un ambiente capace di favorire la condivisione di idee e conoscenze, consentendo ai professionisti di esprimere appieno il proprio potenziale.



Grazie all'impegno nella promozione di un ambiente di lavoro inclusivo, il Network KPMG è conforme ai requisiti della norma **ISO 30415** sul Sistema di Gestione della Diversità e Inclusione e alla **UNI/PdR 125** relativa alla parità di genere.



Nel **KPMG Impact Plan** sono riassunte tutte le azioni concrete che KPMG sta portando avanti sui temi ambientali, sociali e di governance (ESG).


**Unisciti a noi!**



I tuoi dati personali saranno trattati nel pieno rispetto delle disposizioni del **Regolamento (UE) n. 679/2016 (GDPR)** e successive modifiche. Per maggiori informazioni è possibile consultare l'informativa sulla privacy di KPMG.


**KPMG Fides Servizi di Amministrazione S.p.A.** promuove le pari opportunità ed è un datore di lavoro che garantisce l'uguaglianza di trattamento per tutti i candidati.

  


**Do Work That Matters \- Your role and responsibilities**

  


**KPMG Fides Servizi di Amministrazione** provides professional services for administrative, accounting and tax processes. It plays a key role in redesigning reporting models and harmonising management accounting processes.

  



KPMG Fides has over 300 professionals across four locations and continues to grow. As the Italian company of the KPMG network providing professional services for administrative, accounting and tax processes, KPMG Accounting supports leading Italian and international companies with administration, finance and control. It plays a key role in identifying potential opportunities, redesigning reporting models and harmonising management accounting processes. At KPMG, we aim to invest in young people to equip them with the solid skills they need to become trusted, reliable professionals. By joining one of KPMG’s Accounting teams, you will work closely with leading companies, assisting them with an integrated compliance approach to administrative activities to translate the various legislative, regulatory and procedural requirements into accounting, tax and legal processes.

  



Specifically, by joining the **Milan** team, you will be involved in **Payroll/M\&A activities**, including:

  


* In\-depth analysis of the various topics related to employment law, as well as social security and tax;
* Continuous monitoring of Italian and international regulations concerning contracts, social security and tax;
* Support in the analysis of compensation and social security issues related to personnel involved in extraordinary corporate transactions;
* Assist in the monthly payroll processing and in the management of annual and periodic compliance activities;
* Contribute to the payroll delivery process (reporting, etc.).

  




**Come As You Are \- Your skills and characteristics**

  



This is the perfect opportunity for you if you have:


* a degree in business or legal subjects;
* a strong track record in payroll and contributions, preferably in professional firms;
* appropriate training and constant updates with respect to all labour laws and their correct application;
* excellent knowledge of the Microsoft Office suite, specifically Excel;
* flexibility, excellent interpersonal and teamwork skills;
* excellent knowledge of English.

  




**Your Future With Us \- What you’ll find at KPMG**

  


* An **inclusive** and equitable **work environment** that respects, encourages and values **diversity**
* **Hybrid** work arrangements to promote a healthy work\-life balance.
* Advanced **training** and development programmes to stay current.
* **International mindset** and **innovative projects.**
* Initiatives focused on **mental and physical well\-being.**

This opportunity is offered as either an internship or a standard employment contract under the collective labour agreement for the commerce sector, with a starting gross annual salary of €24,000\. The salary is determined on the basis of objective criteria, including educational qualifications and an assessment of the candidate’s profile against the requirements of the role.



In accordance with applicable legislation, we do not collect or use information relating to remuneration received in current or previous employment. Our selection procedures are based on objective and gender\-neutral criteria.

  

**Together for Better – Our values and commitment**



Leadership, teamwork, respect every individual, continuous learning, transparency, commitment to the community and integrity. These are the **KPMG** **values** that create an environment that encourages the sharing of ideas and knowledge, so our professionals can **achieve their full potential**. As a result of our commitment to fostering an **inclusive work** environment, our Network complies with the standards of the **ISO 30415** requirements, on the Diversity and Inclusion Management System, and **UNI PdR 125**, relating to gender equality. Within our **Impact Plan** \- bit.ly/OurImpactPlanKPMG \- you will find summarised all the **concrete actions** we are taking on environmental, social and governance (**ESG**) issues.


**Join us!**



Your data will be processed in full compliance with the provisions of Regulation (EU) no. 679/2016 (General Data Protection Regulation \- “GDPR”), as amended. For more information, please see KPMG’s privacy policy.



KPMG Fides Servizi di Amministrazione S.p.A. is an equal opportunity employer.

title: Senior Surety Underwriter Italy (m/f/d*) - Munich Re Specialty Global Markets (MRS-GM)
description:
**Location** Milan, Italy **Job type** Full\-Time **Work mode** Hybrid **Job level** Professional **Job ID** 13220 **Company** Munich Re Specialty \- Global Markets **Employment type** Regular **Area of expertise** Claims \& Underwriting
  
*Are you ready to shape the future of surety underwriting in Italy?***About Us**
At Munich Re Specialty – Global Markets (MRS\-GM), our ambition is to become the leading Primary Specialty Insurance provider, underpinned by an effective and adaptable strategy, superior products, and industry leaders working in a supportive environment to achieve this.
At the heart of our success is a strong culture where people are encouraged to be present, bold, and curious, allowing them to achieve their individual goals.*Please see our website for more information.***Senior Surety Underwriter Italy**
Munich Re Specialty was formed in 2023 as a stand\-alone division, bringing together the wider Munich Re Group’s primary specialty business units. With strong foundations in the UK and the US, MRS\-GM is expanding its footprint across key European markets.
As part of this growth, we are seeking a \*\*Senior Surety Underwriter – Italy\*\* to support the ramp\-up of our surety business in the Italian market.
This is a unique opportunity to contribute to the development of a new business platform, working closely with the Head of Surety Underwriting Italy and key stakeholders. You will underwrite complex risks, support portfolio development, and help establish Munich Re as a trusted partner in the Italian surety market.
This role will suit a hands\-on, market\-facing professional who thrives in an entrepreneurial environment and enjoys taking ownership of transactions while contributing to the growth of a new business line.**Responsibilities****Underwriting \& Risk Analysis*** Originate, structure, and underwrite complex surety risks, including credit assessments, deal structuring, and financial analysis in line with Munich Re guidelines.
* Independently manage international bonding transactions and ensure alignment with underwriting standards.

**Portfolio Development*** Support the growth of the Italian surety portfolio, monitor performance, and ensure underwriting discipline, compliance, and profitability.

**Claims \& Restructuring*** Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to manage restructuring and claims situations effectively.

**Market Engagement*** Represent Munich Re in the Italian market, building strong relationships with brokers, clients, and industry stakeholders.
* Contribute to identifying market trends, opportunities, and emerging risks.

**Contract \& Governance*** Conduct independent reviews of contract and bond wordings to ensure compliance with internal standards.
* Approve transactions within delegated authority or prepare referrals for higher\-level approval.

**Collaboration \& Execution*** Work closely with the Head of Surety Underwriting Italy and cross\-functional teams including claims, legal, compliance, and finance.
* Support internal training initiatives and knowledge sharing across the organization.
* Contribute to strategic initiatives and projects during the ramp\-up phase.

**Conduct \& Values*** Uphold high standards of integrity, professionalism, and customer focus in line with regulatory expectations.

**Knowledge and Skills*** 5–8 years’ experience in surety underwriting, credit insurance, or banking.
* Strong knowledge of surety products, financial analysis, and credit risk assessment.
* Proven experience in originating, structuring, and underwriting surety transactions.
* Good understanding of financial markets, credit risk, and capital instruments.
* Entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to develop tailored solutions for complex client needs.
* Hands\-on, proactive approach with the ability to work both independently and collaboratively.
* Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
* Fluency in English and Italian; additional European languages are a plus.
* Willingness to travel and work in an international and cross\-cultural environment.

*If you are motivated to play a key role in building Munich Re’s surety presence in Italy, we would be delighted to hear from you.***Diversity, Equity \& Inclusion**
At Munich Re, Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion foster innovation and resilience and enable us to act braver and better. Embracing the power of DEI is at the core of who we are. We recognise diversity can be multi\-dimensional, intersectional, and complex, so we want to build a diverse workforce that includes a wide range of racial, ethnic, sexual, and gender identities; economic and geographic backgrounds; physical abilities; ages; life, school, and career experiences; and political, religious, and personal beliefs. Additionally, we are committed to building an equitable and inclusive work environment where this diversity is celebrated, valued, and has equitable opportunities to succeed.
All candidates in consideration for any role can request a reasonable adjustment at any point in our recruitment process. You can request an adjustment by speaking to your Talent Acquisition contact.**Learning and innovating today, striving for sustainable societies and business tomorrow**
At Munich Re Specialty – Global Markets our approach to ESG is underpinned by our desire to seize business opportunities and to nurture a stimulating and inclusive work environment. Our ESG strategy aims to deliver holistic impacts across environmental, social and governance topics including supporting a number of local initiatives within our community and offering volunteering opportunities for colleagues**Learn more about sustainability at Munich Re** **– choose your impact!****\#BePresent** **\#BeBold** **\#BeCurious**
  
**Your Benefits**
-----------------


With us, you get more than just an exciting job. Enjoy a wide range of employee benefits tailored to your wellbeing and development. Please note that regional differences may apply.


Competitive Salary


We provide fair and competitive compensation that reflects your performance and commitment.


Company performance\-based Incentives


In addition to your salary, our variable compensation approach allows you to share in Munich Re's success.


Recognition and Special Rewards


We recognise outstanding individual contributions through a variety of targeted rewards and incentives.


Retirement Planning


We support your long\-term financial wellbeing with retirement solutions aligned with local regulations.

We foster a respectful, inclusive, and values\-driven environment.


Learning \& Development


We offer tailored learning opportunities with a strong focus on core skills and business\-critical knowledge.


Work\-Life\-Balance


Supporting your ability to balance family, leisure, and your career.


Health \& Wellbeing


We support your physical and mental health through a range of programs and activities in each location.

title: Legal Counsel Digital, AI and Cyber Law, Robotics
description:
At ABB, we help industries outrun \- leaner and cleaner. Here, progress is an expectation \- for you, your team, and the world. As a global market leader, we’ll give you what you need to make it happen. It won’t always be easy, growing takes grit. But at ABB, you’ll never run alone. Run what runs the world.


This role sits within ABB's Robotics business, a leading global robotics company. We're entering an exciting new chapter as we’ve announced the plan for SoftBank Group to acquire ABB Robotics. SoftBank is a globally recognized technology group and investor/operator focused on AI, robotics, and next\-generation computing. By joining us now, you’ll be part of a pioneering team shaping the future of robotics—working alongside world\-class experts in a fast\-moving, innovation\-driven environment.


This Position reports to:


Global Head of IP and Digital, Robotics  

As **Lead Counsel Digital, AI, \& Cyber Law**, you will play a pivotal role in supporting the company’s digital business and transformation initiatives reporting directly to the Head of IP. You will partner with business leaders, technology teams, and group functions to provide practical legal and compliance guidance, ensuring that innovation and growth in digital solutions are underpinned by sound legal frameworks and integrity standards.  

  

The work model for the role is: hybrid \#LI\-hybrid  

The location for the role is flexible in Europe and US.

 **Your responsibilities:**

* Provide legal advice and support on digital business initiatives, including AI, cloud services, datacentre, software, licensing, and technology\-driven solutions.
* Responsible for guiding the company’s digital strategy, leveraging AI and emerging technologies.
* Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of digital and technology\-related agreements with global customers, partners, and suppliers.
* Support digital transformation projects, ensuring compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and internal policies.
* Advise on intellectual property, cybersecurity, and related regulatory issues impacting digital business.
* Collaborate with global and local Legal \& Integrity colleagues to ensure consistency of approach across jurisdictions.
* Monitor legal and regulatory trends in digital and technology markets, proactively identifying risks and opportunities.
* Protect the company’s reputation by ensuring operations meet ethical and compliance standards.
* Provide training and guidance to business stakeholders on legal issues relevant to digital offerings.
 **Your qualifications:**

* Qualified lawyer with 10–15 years of experience in commercial/technology law, preferably gained in a multinational corporate or top\-tier law firm environment.
* Strong expertise in digital, AI, IT, cyber law, software licensing, cloud, data centre, and data protection law.
* Previous experience in Robotics sector is preferred but not required.
* Proven track record in negotiating complex technology agreements across international markets.
* Excellent communication and collaboration skills, able to engage effectively with senior business stakeholders.
* Pragmatic, solution\-oriented, and commercially minded, with the ability to balance innovation and risk.
* Fluency in English required; additional European languages are a strong asset.

We value people from different backgrounds. Could this be your story? Apply today or visit www.abb.com to read more about us and learn about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: Cyber Security Intern
description:
**Job Description:**

DXC Technology is looking for a **Cyber Security Intern** to join **Risk \& Data Governance projects.**

**Responsibilities**

You will work within our **Cyber Risk \& Governance practice** , contributing to regulatory, compliance, and risk‑management activities across enterprise environments.


Your work will focus on major European regulatory frameworks, including: **DORA** and **NIS2**

You will support:

title: Finance Business Partner
description:
Welcome to Haleon. We’re a purpose\-driven, world\-class consumer company putting everyday health in the hands of millions. In just three years since our launch, we’ve grown, evolved and are now entering an exciting new chapter – one filled with bold ambitions and enormous opportunity.
  

  

Our trusted portfolio of brands – including Sensodyne®, Panadol®, Advil®, Voltaren®, Theraflu®, Otrivin®, and Centrum® – lead in resilient and growing categories. What sets us apart is our unique blend of deep human understanding and trusted science.
  

  

Now it’s time to fully realise the full potential of our business and our people. We do this through our Win as One strategy. It puts our purpose – to deliver better everyday health with humanity – at the heart of everything we do. It unites us, inspires us, and challenges us to be better every day, driven by our agile, performance\-focused culture.


The Finance Business Partner will work at country level and will report to Head of Finance Italy, within the Southern Europe Finance team. He/she will support the P\&L monthly planning \& forecasting process and business decisions taking through value\-adding financial analyses.


It is essential that the candidate has good communication skills in order to be able to operate effectively in a challenging environment, as well as the capability of creating strong cross\-functional relationships, combined with leadership, proactivity, learning agility, resilience and very strong commitment to meet deadlines and finalize the execution of deliverables.


**Key responsibilities**

* P\&L management: Planning and forecasting of Net Sales, Advertising \& Promotions and Overheads lines
* Partnering with Marketing team in business decisions to match the proper Brand contribution
* Partnering with Comex team to better shape reports, analysis and areas of improvement
* Partnering with Supply chain to match financial targets in P\&L
* Supporting the Finance Hub team in Month End Closure activities

**Qualifications and skills**

* Master Degree in Economics, Engineering or similar.
* Previous relevant experience (\+2 years) in a Finance Business Partner, Sales/Business Analytics, Strategy or Business Development role, preferably in a large FMCG organization.
* High level of commercial acumen and very high level of analytical skills, with a Finance background preferred.
* Fluency in spoken and written English.
* Proficient in office package (with special focus on excel and powerpoint).
* Market data analysis and business understanding.

Please save a copy of the job description, as this may be helpful to refer to once the advert closes.  



\&\#xa;EUR 40\.632,00 \- EUR 55\.869,00\&\#xa;\&\#xa;The salary range for this position is indicative and provided as a guide. The final offer may vary depending on factors including relevant experience, skills, qualifications, and internal equity considerations. Further details regarding the total reward and benefits package will be shared during the recruitment process. Salary ranges may vary by location in line with local market conditions and applicable pay transparency regulations.\&\#xa;\&\#xa;Compensation information is shared for the primary location of this role. Where the position is advertised with additional locations the compensation range will be discussed during the recruiting process.  

**Job Posting End Date**

2026\-08\-01  

**Equal Opportunities**
-----------------------

Haleon are committed to mobilising our purpose in a way that represents the diverse consumers and communities who rely on our brands every day. It guides us in creating an inclusive culture, where different backgrounds and views are valued and respected – all in support of understanding and best serving the needs of our consumers and unleashing the full potential of our people. It’s important to us that Haleon is a place where all our employees feel they truly belong.
  

  

During the application process, we may ask you to share some personal information, which is entirely voluntary. This information ensures we meet certain regulatory and reporting obligations and supports the development, refinement, and execution of our inclusion and belonging programmes that are open to all Haleon employees.
  

  

The personal information you provide will be kept confidential, used only for legitimate business purposes, and will never be used in making any employment decisions, including hiring decisions.  



**Adjustment or Accommodations Request**
----------------------------------------

If you require a reasonable adjustment or accommodation or other assistance to apply for a job at Haleon at any stage of the application process, please let your recruiter know by providing them with a description of specific adjustments you are requesting. We’ll provide all reasonable adjustments to support you throughout the recruitment process and treat all information you provide us in confidence.  



**Note to candidates**
----------------------

The Haleon recruitment team will contact you using a Haleon email account (@haleon.com). If you are not sure whether the email you received is from Haleon, please get in touch.
